question_text: A student shines light through a prism and observes the visible light spectrum displayed on a white screen. Which of the following statements is correct about the light that has been separated?

options.label: A
options.text: Red light has a shorter wavelength than violet light, so it bends more in the prism.

options.label: B
options.text: Violet light has a shorter wavelength than red light, so it bends more when passing through the prism.

options.label: C
options.text: All colours of light bend by the same amount because they are all part of white light.

options.label: D
options.text: Blue light bends the least because it is in the middle of the visible spectrum.

explanation: Different colours of light have different wavelengths. Violet light has the shortest wavelength in the visible spectrum, and shorter wavelengths bend more when they pass through a prism. This is why violet appears at one end of the spectrum and red at the other.
